economic_notes: Band operates multiple business enterprises including Ainsworth Hot Springs, Lower Kootenay Development LP, Morris Flowers Inc., and yaqan nukiy Farms LP. Subsequent to year end, Band purchased Armstrong Property for $4,650,597 through Lower Kootenay Development Corporation. Settlement claim of $500,000 awarded subsequent to year end and distributed to members.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Population stated as 'over 200 members'. One fully consolidated subsidiary (Lower Kootenay Guide Outfitters Ltd.) and 7 entities on modified equity basis (GBEs). Total government transfers calculated as ISC $2,068,584 + FNESC $335,133 + FNHA $866,478 + Province of BC $1,976,998 + CMHC $17,259 = $5,264,452. OSR = $9,121,748 - $5,264,452 = $3,857,296 (includes rental income, other income, and income from business enterprises). No remuneration schedule included in the document. Subsequent events: $4,650,597 property purchase and $500,000 settlement claim distributed to members after year end. Ktunaxa Nation Council revenue was $2,500,833 in 2021 but $0 in 2022.
